events.title: Rome Controls North Africa
events.description: Following Octavian's victory, the entire North African coastline is firmly under Roman control.

events.title: Pentecost
events.description: Jews from Cyrene are present in Jerusalem and witness the descent of the Holy Spirit.

events.title: Martyrdom of the Scillitan Twelve
events.description: Twelve Christians from Scillium are executed for refusing to swear by the emperor's genius.

events.title: Martyrdom of Perpetua and Felicitas
events.description: Noblewoman Perpetua and her servant Felicitas are martyred in the Amphitheater of Carthage.

events.title: Council of Carthage (Canon)
events.description: The Council produces a canonical list of the 27 books of the New Testament.

events.title: Augustine Writes The City of God
events.description: Augustine authors his monumental work in response to the sack of Rome.

events.title: Vandal Invasion
events.description: Arian Vandals led by Gaiseric cross into North Africa, bringing severe persecution.

events.title: Fall of Carthage to Arab Forces
events.description: Arab forces capture and destroy Carthage, effectively ending the organized church in North Africa.
